text.title: C# Background Services Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules grounding ASP.NET Core BackgroundService + Channels patterns. Each rule pairs binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Long-running in-process work MUST extend `BackgroundService` (never `Task.Run(async () => while(true) ...)` and never implement `IHostedService` directly).

rationale.source: Microsoft Learn — Background tasks with hosted services

rule.rationale: `BackgroundService` plumbs `ExecuteAsync`, `StartAsync`, `StopAsync`, and `stoppingToken`. `Task.Run` bypasses `IHost` lifecycle, cancellation, and DI scoping.

rule.statement: Every `await` inside `ExecuteAsync` MUST receive `stoppingToken` (or a linked token). No `await Task.Delay(ms)` without the token; no `await channel.Reader.ReadAllAsync()` without the token.
rule.rationale: Without `stoppingToken`, shutdown blocks for the default 30s `ShutdownTimeout` before SIGKILL; in-flight work is force-killed.

rule.statement: The per-item handler inside `ExecuteAsync` MUST be wrapped in `try/catch`. Unhandled exceptions MUST NOT bubble to `ExecuteAsync`'s outer loop.
rule.rationale: Default `BackgroundServiceExceptionBehavior.StopHost` brings down the whole process on an unhandled `ExecuteAsync` exception.

rule.statement: Scoped services (`DbContext`, scoped repositories) MUST be resolved via `IServiceProvider.CreateScope()` inside the per-item loop. Scoped types MUST NOT appear in the `BackgroundService` constructor.

rationale.source: EF Core docs — DbContext lifetime + .NET DI captive-dependency warnings

rule.rationale: `BackgroundService` is a singleton; injecting `DbContext` directly creates a captive dependency reused across items, causing concurrency bugs and connection leaks.

rule.statement: In-memory work queues MUST use `Channel.CreateBounded<T>(new BoundedChannelOptions(capacity) { FullMode = BoundedChannelFullMode.Wait })` (or `DropOldest` when lossy is acceptable). `Channel.CreateUnbounded` is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Unbounded channels under producer spikes exhaust memory. Bounded + Wait propagates back-pressure to the producer.
